prompt: The relation 'is greater than' (>) on the numbers is:
hint: Is anything greater than itself? If x > y, is y > x?
explanation: '>' is irreflexive (no number is greater than itself, so ¬(x > x)) and transitive (if x > y and y > z then x > z). It is also asymmetric. Together these make it a STRICT order — the opposite profile from an equivalence relation.
